The pulse-broadening timescale ($\tau_{\text{MW}}$) and scintillation bandwidth ($\Delta\nu_{\text{MW}}$) are scaled from the NE2025 output at 1\,GHz to the center of the CHIME/FRB band (600\,MHz) and the DSA-110 band (1.4\,GHz) assuming Kolmogorov scaling ($\tau \propto \nu^{-4.4}$ and $\Delta\nu_d \propto \nu^{4.4}$).

Yellow stars indicate the positions of the twelve sightlines. Bottom row: Individual sightline values plotted against absolute Galactic latitude $|b|$ for dispersion (left), scaled scattering timescale (middle; CHIME at 600\,MHz in orange, DSA-110 at 1.4\,GHz in blue), and scaled scintillation bandwidth (right). \FloatBarrier
\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}
\label{sec:results-scintillation}

% TODO(results-scintillation): Insert after the scintillation products and
% scattering fits are re-run under the re-validation contract. This subsection
% should connect the decorrelation-bandwidth measurements, single-screen tests,
% and screen-attribution claims only after both ingredients are trusted.
%
% Required before filling:
% - Re-run DSA-band scintillation products under the validation contract.
% - Complete CHIME-band scintillation campaign where needed.
% - Re-establish the scattering fits used in any tau * Delta-nu product.
% - Decide whether scintillation acts as a geometry-selection constraint or as
%   part of a fitted two-screen model.
%
% Candidate future artifact, once trusted:
% - fig:scint_screens

\FloatBarrier
\subsection{Turbulence spectrum and burst multiplicity}
\label{sec:results-alpha}

% TODO(results-turbulence): Insert only after the joint scattering fits pass
% the fit re-validation ladder and the geometry-selection campaign has decided
% which model family is citable per sightline. Do not quote rail classes,
% alpha=4 limits, class fractions, median beta, or campaign-era fit values as
% sky results.
%
% This slot should eventually report:
% - the validated per-sightline turbulence parameters, if any;
% - the model family under which each value is citable;
% - multiplicity decisions that survive re-validation;
% - any morphology-induced bias demonstration that is re-established.
%
% Candidate future artifacts, once trusted:
% - tab:beta
% - fig:whitney_mult

The component-multiplicity audit is already informative as a validation step,
even before the turbulence parameters are restored as citable measurements.
Figure~\ref{fig:jointmodel-pair-whitney} shows the current FRB~20220310F
two-dimensional data/model/residual panel after the CHIME
side is forced to retain its resolved second pulse. The full set of eleven
current joint-model morphology-audit panels is included in
Appendix~\ref{app:jointmodel-pairs}. We use these figures to check whether the
adopted component count leaves coherent burst power in the residuals; we do not
yet quote the fitted scattering or turbulence parameters from them.

name = "flits"

# Guarded/function-level imports: core code runs without these and degrades

nested = ["dynesty>=2.1"]                                   # nested sampling / Bayesian evidence (burstfit_nested.py)
galactic = ["mwprop>=0.1.0", "mpmath>=1.0.0", "pygedm>=3.3.0", "setuptools<81"]  # NE2001/YMW16/NE2025 Galactic DM; mwprop needs mpmath (undeclared by mwprop); pygedm needs pkg_resources (setuptools<81) + a manual macOS build, see environment.yml

perf = ["numba>=0.54.0", "joblib>=1.1.0"]                   # optional JIT + parallelism (analysis.py, simulation/engine.py)

[dependency-groups]

]

]

  "ruff>=0.15.18",
]

[project.scripts]

=== results.tex lines 1-50 ===
\section{Results}
\label{sec:results}

The association tests, the foreground census, and the dispersion-budget
decomposition are reported below. The scattering fits for individual sightlines,
the scintillation products, the turbulence parameters, the burst energies, and
the measured-versus-predicted scattering comparison are presented in the
following subsections.

\FloatBarrier
\subsection{Association of the co-detections}
\label{sec:results-association}

All twelve candidate pairs pass the association tests of
Section~\ref{sec:toa}. Under the conservative, chance-maximizing windows of
Section~\ref{sec:toa-pcc}, every pair has a chance-coincidence probability
$P_{\rm cc}<10^{-8}$---fewer than one in a hundred million such pairings would be
expected to arise by chance---and passes the positional-coincidence check; the
timing residuals, referenced to
400\,MHz under the shared DSA-DM convention, are consistent with the
predicted inter-site geometric delay (Table~\ref{tab:sample}). The eight
bursts with constrained CHIME-side dispersion measures also pass the
CHIME--DSA DM-agreement check; the remaining four are retained as
position-and-timing associations, since their CHIME sub-band fits do not
provide a constraining independent DM. We therefore treat all twelve pairs as
genuine co-detections in what follows.

\FloatBarrier
\subsection{Per-sightline dispersion budget}
\label{sec:results-budget}
\label{sec:budget}

Table~\ref{tab:budget} reports the decomposition of Eq.~\ref{eq:dmbudget}
along the twelve sightlines. Three features organize the sample. First, the
intervening term is strongly concentrated: summed over the confirmed
foreground systems of Section~\ref{sec:obs-fg}, $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$ exceeds
$100\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ on only one sightline (FRB~20230307A,
$241\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$, dominated by the intracluster column of
Section~\ref{sec:dominant-systems}), while the remaining sightlines carry
$\lesssim 85\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ and four tabulate
$\mathrm{DM_{int}}=0$---for two distinct reasons. FRB~20220506D and
FRB~20240122A lie outside the deep-imaging footprints, so their sightlines
are constrained only by the shallow all-sky layers and their intervening
terms are unconstrained rather than measured to be zero (note~u). FRB~20240203A
and FRB~20240229A carry confirmed foreground halos, but those halos lie
beyond the virial radii implied by their assumed masses, so the modeled
columns vanish; their zeros are conditional on the mass assumption (note~m),
and FRB~20240203A is additionally outside the deep footprints. One sightline
(FRB~20230814B) crosses a foreground halo in the
galaxy-interior regime ($b \approx 4.6$\,kpc), where the reported column is

=== DSA_LORENTZIAN_FITS.md (burst overview table region) === # DSA Lorentzian ACF Fit Summary Fresh DSA ACFs were computed from the staged `.npz` dynamic spectra. Each sub-band was fit with 1, 2, and 3 Lorentzian components; adding a component required both strong BIC improvement and the nested-F test threshold in the existing `compare_lorentzian_components` selector. The number of DSA sub-bands is selected within this run, not inherited from the checked-in burst YAML. For each burst the driver evaluates 2, 3, and 4 equal-S/N frequency splits, then chooses the largest candidate for which every produced sub-band passes fixed viability gates: at least 512 unmasked channels, at least an 8 MHz fitted lag window, and at least 30 positive-lag fit samples, with at least one selected component not carrying a quality flag. If no candidate satisfies all gates, the least pathological candidate is retained and the fallback policy is recorded. ## Burst Overview | burst | selected subbands | preferred n by subband | plurality n | median dnu by component (MHz) | selection note | |---|---:|---|---:|---|---| | casey | 4 | [1, 2, 1, 2] | 1 | c1=3.227, c2=18.39 | rejected n=3: subband 0 has no unflagged selected component | | chromatica | 4 | [1, 2, 1, 2] | 1 | c1=1.059 | largest viable candidate | | freya | 2 | [1, 1] | 1 | c1=11.91 | rejected n=2: subband 1 has no unflagged selected component
n=3: subband 0 has no unflagged selected component

n=3: subband 0 has no unflagged selected component

n=3: subband 0 has no unflagged selected component

Filled circles are clean selected Lorentzian bandwidth measurements; dashed guides are shown only when at least two distinct clean sub-band frequencies anchor the fixed $\gamma\propto\nu^4$ scaling. Selected components with quality flags remain in the tables and per-burst diagnostics. ![DSA Lorentzian bandwidth summary](figures/dsa_lorentzian_summary.png) ## Component Rows === model figure env in observations.tex === (ISM) column alone. In the dispersion budget of Section~\ref{sec:dm} (Table~\ref{tab:budget}) we add the $40\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ Milky-Way halo prior to this disk term, so the $\mathrm{DM_{MW}}$ column there ($74$--$137\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$) is larger than the $\lesssim95\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ disk-only values here by that fixed halo offset; the two are consistent once the halo term is accounted for. \begin{figure*} \centering \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/ne2025_mw_characterization_nside32.pdf} \caption{NE2025 Galactic electron density model predictions. Top row: All-sky Mollweide maps (in Galactic coordinates) showing predicted dispersion measure ($\text{DM}_{\text{MW}}$, left), pulse-broadening timescale ($\tau_{\text{MW}}$ at 1\,GHz, middle), and scintillation bandwidth ($\Delta\nu_{\text{MW}}$ at 1\,GHz, right). Yellow stars indicate the positions of the twelve sightlines. Bottom row: Individual sightline values plotted against absolute Galactic latitude $|b|$ for dispersion (left), scaled scattering timescale (middle; CHIME at 600\,MHz in orange, DSA-110 at 1.4\,GHz in blue), and scaled scintillation bandwidth (right). Scatter plots show values calculated assuming Kolmogorov scaling ($\alpha = 4.4$) \citep{Ocker2026}.} \label{fig:ne2025_mw} \end{figure*} \subsection{Foreground-galaxy search} \label{sec:obs-fg}

sections/observations.tex:52:To characterize the expected propagation effects contributed by the Milky Way, we query the NE2025 Galactic electron density model \citep{Ocker2026} along each of the twelve co-detection sightlines. The expected Galactic dispersion measure ($\text{DM}_{\text{MW}}$) is integrated to a distance of 30\,kpc. The pulse-broadening timescale ($\tau_{\text{MW}}$) and scintillation bandwidth ($\Delta\nu_{\text{MW}}$) are scaled from the NE2025 output at 1\,GHz to the center of the CHIME/FRB band (600\,MHz) and the DSA-110 band (1.4\,GHz) assuming Kolmogorov scaling ($\tau \propto \nu^{-4.4}$ and $\Delta\nu_d \propto \nu^{4.4}$).

source:
import csv
from collections import defaultdict
csvp="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/dsa_lorentzian_components.csv"
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open(csvp)))
clean=[r for r in rows if not r["quality_flags"]]
by=defaultdict(list)
for r in clean: by[r["burst"]].append(r)
# n bursts with >=1 clean, and with >=2 distinct clean freqs (guide condition)
n_bursts=len(by)
dnu_all=[float(r["dnu_mhz"]) for r in clean]
print("bursts with >=1 clean:", n_bursts, "of 12")
print("total clean components:", len(clean), "of", len(rows))
print("dnu range MHz: %.3g – %.3g" % (min(dnu_all), max(dnu_all)))
guide_bursts=[]; single_bursts=[]
for b,rs in by.items():
    freqs=set(round(float(r["center_freq_mhz"]),3) for r in rs)
    (guide_bursts if len(freqs)>=2 else single_bursts).append(b)
print("bursts with >=2 distinct clean freqs (guide shown):", len(guide_bursts), sorted(guide_bursts))
print("bursts with single clean freq (no guide):", len(single_bursts), sorted(single_bursts))
# narrowest / broadest clean median per burst
import statistics
med={b:statistics.median(float(r["dnu_mhz"]) for r in rs) for b,rs in by.items()}
print("per-burst clean median dnu (MHz):")
for b in sorted(med, key=med.get): print(f"  {b:12s} {med[b]:.3g}  (nclean={len(by[b])})")

# [edit_file] /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/results.tex
--- /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/results.tex
+++ /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/results.tex
-\FloatBarrier
-\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}
-\label{sec:results-scintillation}
-
-% TODO(results-scintillation): Insert after the scintillation products and
-% scattering fits are re-run under the re-validation contract. This subsection
-% should connect the decorrelation-bandwidth measurements, single-screen tests,
-% and screen-attribution claims only after both ingredients are trusted.
-%
-% Required before filling:
-% - Re-run DSA-band scintillation products under the validation contract.
-% - Complete CHIME-band scintillation campaign where needed.
-% - Re-establish the scattering fits used in any tau * Delta-nu product.
-% - Decide whether scintillation acts as a geometry-selection constraint or as
-%   part of a fitted two-screen model.
-%
-% Candidate future artifact, once trusted:
-% - fig:scint_screens
-
-\FloatBarrier
+\FloatBarrier
+\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}
+\label{sec:results-scintillation}
+
+We report here the DSA-band diffractive-scintillation products that have been
+re-derived under the validation contract; the CHIME-band scintillation
+campaign, the scattering fits that enter any $\tau\,\Delta\nu_d$ screen
+product, and the resulting one- versus two-screen attribution are deferred to
+Section~\ref{sec:disc-screen-attribution} once their prerequisite strands are
+independently trusted.
+
+For each burst we recompute the spectral autocorrelation function (ACF) of the
+DSA-110 dynamic spectrum in equal-signal-to-noise frequency sub-bands and fit
+it with a sum of Lorentzian components, whose half-width at half-maximum defines
+the diffractive decorrelation bandwidth $\gamma$
+\citep{Rickett1990, Cordes2016}. The number of sub-bands and the number of
+Lorentzian components per sub-band are selected internally, from fixed
+model-comparison and viability gates rather than inherited fit choices, as
+described in Section~\ref{sec:methods}; components that fail the bandwidth,
+modulation, or fractional-uncertainty quality flags are retained in the
+component tables but excluded from the summary measurements.
+
+All twelve co-detections yield at least one clean (unflagged) DSA-band
+decorrelation-bandwidth measurement, for a total of 41 clean Lorentzian
+components across the sample. The per-burst clean-component median $\gamma$
+spans roughly $0.24\,$MHz (FRB~20230913A) to $23\,$MHz (FRB~20220310F), with
+individual clean components ranging from $\sim0.13$ to $\sim23\,$MHz across the
+$1.28$--$1.53\,$GHz DSA band---comparable to and in several cases exceeding the
+Galactic diffractive bandwidths predicted by NE2025 along these sightlines
+(Figure~\ref{fig:ne2025_mw}). Figure~\ref{fig:dsa_scint_gamma} summarizes the
+clean measurements as one bandwidth-versus-frequency panel per burst. For the
+nine bursts with clean measurements at two or more distinct sub-band
+frequencies, we overlay a fixed $\gamma\propto\nu^4$ guide anchored to the
+measurements; the three single-frequency bursts (FRB~20230325A, FRB~20221113A,
+and FRB~20220310F) carry no guide, since a single frequency does not constrain
+the scaling. We do not fit a per-burst scaling index here: the frequency lever
+arm within the DSA band alone is short, and a physically meaningful measurement
+of the scaling requires the CHIME-band anchor, which is deferred with the rest
+of the two-band analysis.
+
+\begin{figure*}
+    \centering
+    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/dsa_lorentzian_summary.pdf}
+    \caption{DSA-110 diffractive decorrelation bandwidths for the twelve
+    co-detections. Each panel shows the clean (unflagged) Lorentzian
+    decorrelation bandwidth $\gamma$ against DSA sub-band center frequency for
+    one burst, on a logarithmic bandwidth axis. Filled purple circles are clean
+    selected components with $1\sigma$ fit uncertainties; the dashed gray guide
+    is the fixed $\gamma\propto\nu^4$ scaling anchored to the measurements,
+    shown only for the nine bursts constrained at two or more distinct clean
+    sub-band frequencies. Components carrying quality flags are excluded here but
+    retained in the component tables. The full per-burst ACF diagnostics, showing
+    the stacked sub-band autocorrelation functions with their fitted total
+    Lorentzian models, are provided in
+    Appendix~\ref{app:dsa-scint-acf}.}
+    \label{fig:dsa_scint_gamma}
+\end{figure*}
+
+\FloatBarrier

cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026
echo "=== appendix.tex ==="
cat sections/appendix.tex
echo; echo "=== how main.tex includes appendices ==="
grep -n "appendix\|input{sections\|jointmodel_pairs\|association_cards" main.tex
stdout:
=== appendix.tex ===
\section{Association cards}
\label{app:assoc-cards}

This appendix collects the per-burst association-card figures referenced in the
main text. Each card is a compact, at-a-glance cross-check that the CHIME/FRB and
DSA-110 triggers correspond to the same astrophysical burst, and that the
association used throughout the manuscript is internally consistent.

Across the sample, the cards summarize (i) the timing consistency between the
two pipelines after accounting for barycentric corrections and dispersion, (ii)
sky-position consistency between the candidate localizations (to the extent
available for each burst), and (iii) dispersion-measure consistency and
bookkeeping (e.g., the CHIME/FRB-reported DM compared to any independently
reconstructed DM used in downstream analysis). Many cards also include catalog
context (nearby galaxies, foreground structures, or survey footprints) that is
used as a qualitative sanity check on the association and as a pointer to the
foreground-census layer described in the main text.

When reading the cards, the most important failure modes they are designed to
catch are: a mis-identified counterpart between instruments, a bookkeeping
mismatch (wrong TOA, wrong DM, or wrong metadata version), or an association
that is only explainable by implausible timing/DM offsets. We treat the cards as
validation artifacts: they motivate which bursts advance to the joint
DM/scattering analysis, but we avoid over-interpreting any individual panel
beyond confirming that the association inputs are coherent.

\clearpage
\input{sections/association_cards.tex}
\clearpage

\FloatBarrier

\section{Intracluster dispersion for foreground clusters}
\label{app:clusters}

This appendix collects the per-cluster hot-gas dispersion columns behind the
$b\le R_{500}$ budget cut described in the main text. Only the single cluster
that pierces the halo enters $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$; the remaining panels show the
excluded clusters and the near-miss that motivate the cut being a principled
impact-parameter threshold rather than a hand-picked selection.

\begin{figure*}
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/clusters_icm.pdf}
    \caption{Predicted intracluster hot-gas dispersion for the four foreground
    clusters along the FRB~20230307A sightline. Each panel plots the
    modified-NFW hot-baryon dispersion column $\mathrm{DM}(b)$ against projected
    impact parameter $b$ (blue); the vertical marker is the sightline's measured
    impact parameter, the dashed line is $R_{500}$, and the shaded region is
    inside $R_{500}$. Only J115120.4$+$714435 ($b/R_{500}=0.83$) meets the
    $b\le R_{500}$ budget cut and enters $\mathrm{DM_{int}}$
    ($\sim$160\,pc\,cm$^{-3}$ from this mNFW profile, factor-of-two uncertain across
    profile and cluster-parameter systematics,
    Appendix~\ref{app:host-forward-model}; Table~\ref{tab:foreground}); the
    others lie beyond
    $R_{500}$ and are excluded even where the profile remains formally nonzero
    (J115128.2$+$713637 at $b/R_{500}=1.25$, $\sim$95\,pc\,cm$^{-3}$). Cluster
    masses ($\log M_{500}$) and radii are from the \citet{WenHan2024} DESI
    Legacy/WISE catalog.}
    \label{fig:clusters_icm}
\end{figure*}

\clearpage
\FloatBarrier

\section{Forward-modeled host dispersion}
\label{app:host-forward-model}

The host term of Table~\ref{tab:budget} is a forward-modeled posterior rather
than an arithmetic residual (Section~\ref{sec:dm}). For each redshift-constrained
sightline we draw $N=2\times10^{5}$ Monte-Carlo samples of
$\mathrm{DM_{host}}=\mathrm{DM_{obs}}-\mathrm{DM_{MW,ISM}}-\mathrm{DM_{MW,halo}}
-\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}-\mathrm{DM_{int}}$, propagating priors on the non-host
terms:
\begin{itemize}
\item \emph{Cosmological.} $\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}=\Delta\,\langle\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}
\rangle(z)$ with $\Delta$ from the \citet{Macquart2020} probability density
$p(\Delta)\propto\Delta^{-\beta'}\exp[-(\Delta^{-\alpha'}-C_0)^2/(2\alpha'^2
\sigma_{\rm DM}^2)]$, $\alpha'=\beta'=3$, $\sigma_{\rm DM}=F\,z^{-1/2}$, and $F$
uniform on $[0.25,0.40]$ \citep{McQuinn2014, James2022}; $C_0$ is fixed so
$\langle\Delta\rangle=1$.
\item \emph{Galactic disk.} The NE2025 disk column with a $30\%$ lognormal
uncertainty.
\item \emph{Galactic halo.} $40\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$ median, lognormal with a
factor-$\sim\!2$ span ($2\sigma$ range $\approx[20,80]\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$;
\citealt{YamasakiTotani2020, KeatingPen2020, Cook2023}).
\item \emph{Intervening.} The Table~\ref{tab:foreground} column with a $40\%$
(measured-mass halo), factor-$2$ (assumed-mass halo), or $30\%$ (cluster)
lognormal uncertainty.
\end{itemize}
The resulting posteriors are broad and asymmetric, dominated at higher redshift
by the cosmological scatter (Figure~\ref{fig:dm_host_posteriors},
Table~\ref{tab:host-forward-model}); their medians exceed the mean-subtracted
residuals because $P(\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}})$ is right-skewed. The prior widths
above are fiducial and are documented so they can be revised; the analysis and
both artifacts are reproduced by \texttt{scripts/dm\_budget\_uncertainty.py}.

\begin{deluxetable}{lccc}
\tabletypesize{\footnotesize}
\tablecaption{Forward-modeled host-dispersion posteriors for the nine
redshift-constrained sightlines: median with $16$th--$84$th-percentile interval,
and $P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)$. DM in $\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$.
\label{tab:host-forward-model}}
\tablehead{\colhead{Burst} & \colhead{$z$} & \colhead{$\mathrm{DM_{host}}$} &
  \colhead{$P(\mathrm{DM_{host}}<0)$}}
\startdata
FRB 20220207C & $0.043$ & $51^{+37}_{-49}$   & $0.15$ \\
FRB 20220310F & $0.479$ & $12^{+69}_{-167}$  & $0.45$ \\
FRB 20220506D & $0.300$ & $61^{+54}_{-117}$  & $0.26$ \\
FRB 20221113A & $0.251$ & $65^{+54}_{-102}$  & $0.23$ \\
FRB 20221203A & $0.510$ & $14^{+97}_{-196}$  & $0.46$ \\
FRB 20230307A & $0.271$ & $95^{+87}_{-138}$  & $0.23$ \\
FRB 20230913A & $0.302$ & $146^{+61}_{-123}$ & $0.14$ \\
FRB 20240203A & $0.074$ & $109^{+29}_{-45}$  & $0.04$ \\
FRB 20240229A & $0.287$ & $211^{+46}_{-112}$ & $0.08$ \\
\enddata
\end{deluxetable}

\begin{figure*}
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/dm_host_posteriors.pdf}
    \caption{\emph{Left:} forward-modeled host-dispersion posteriors (median and
    $16$th--$84$th-percentile interval, dark points) for the nine
    redshift-constrained sightlines, compared with the arithmetic
    mean-subtracted residual (orange crosses). Sampling the full, skewed
    $P(\mathrm{DM_{cosmic}}\,|\,z)$ shifts the posterior median above the
    mean-subtracted value and turns the two formerly negative residuals
    (FRB 20220310F, FRB 20221203A) into posteriors consistent with zero.
    \emph{Right:} the FRB 20230307A intracluster column from an X-ray-motivated
    isothermal $\beta$-model Monte Carlo (over $M_{500}$, $f_{\rm gas}$, and
    profile shape); the vertical line marks the two-phase mNFW value
    ($\sim\!160\,\mathrm{pc\,cm^{-3}}$) adopted in the budget.}
    \label{fig:dm_host_posteriors}
\end{figure*}

\clearpage
\FloatBarrier

\section{Joint-model morphology audits}
\label{app:jointmodel-pairs}

This appendix collects the per-burst two-dimensional data/model/residual panels
for the current joint-model morphology audit. The panels are included to make
the component-multiplicity and residual checks visible in the manuscript, while
leaving the fit-derived turbulence and scattering parameters deferred to the
completed scattering analysis.

\clearpage
\input{sections/jointmodel_pairs.tex}
\clearpage

\FloatBarrier

import re, os
root="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026"
def read(p): return open(os.path.join(root,p)).read()
results = read("sections/results.tex")
appendix = read("sections/appendix.tex")
panels = read("sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex")

checks=[]
# 1. figure* balance in results.tex
checks.append(("results figure* balanced", results.count(r"\begin{figure*}")==results.count(r"\end{figure*}")))
# 2. new label defined
checks.append(("fig:dsa_scint_gamma defined", r"\label{fig:dsa_scint_gamma}" in results))
checks.append(("app:dsa-scint-acf defined", r"\label{app:dsa-scint-acf}" in appendix))
# 3. refs resolve to labels somewhere
alltex="".join(read(f"sections/{f}") for f in os.listdir(os.path.join(root,"sections")) if f.endswith(".tex"))
alltex += read("main.tex")
labels=set(re.findall(r"\\label\{([^}]+)\}", alltex))
refs=set(re.findall(r"\\(?:ref|autoref|cref)\{([^}]+)\}", alltex))
# refs used in my new text
mynew_refs={"fig:dsa_scint_gamma","app:dsa-scint-acf","fig:ne2025_mw","sec:methods","sec:disc-screen-attribution"}
for r_ in sorted(mynew_refs):
    checks.append((f"ref {r_} has label", r_ in labels))
# 4. panel macro defined & used
checks.append(("panel macro defined", r"\newcommand{\dsascintacfpanel}" in panels))
checks.append(("12 panel invocations", panels.count(r"\dsascintacfpanel{")==13))  # 1 def line contains name too
# 5. citations exist in bib
bib=read("bib/refs.bib")
for key in ["Rickett1990","Cordes2016"]:
    checks.append((f"cite {key} in bib", ("{"+key+",") in bib))
# 6. appendix inputs the panel file
checks.append(("appendix inputs panel file", r"\input{sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex}" in appendix))
# 7. figure files exist
for f in ["figures/dsa_lorentzian_summary.pdf"]+[f"figures/dsa_scint_acf/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf" for b in ["casey","zach"]]:
    checks.append((f"file {f}", os.path.exists(os.path.join(root,f))))

for name,ok in checks:
    print(("PASS" if ok else "FAIL"), name)
print("\nALL PASS:", all(ok for _,ok in checks))

import re, os
root="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026"
sec=os.path.join(root,"sections")
files=["main.tex"]+[f"sections/{f}" for f in os.listdir(sec) if f.endswith(".tex")]
texts={f:open(os.path.join(root,f)).read() for f in files}
allt="\n".join(texts.values())

# duplicate labels
labels=re.findall(r"\\label\{([^}]+)\}", allt)
dupes=[l for l in set(labels) if labels.count(l)>1]
print("duplicate labels:", dupes or "none")

# undefined refs (referenced but no label) — only count those in included files
labelset=set(labels)
refs=re.findall(r"\\(?:ref|autoref|Cref|cref|eqref)\{([^}]+)\}", allt)
undef=sorted(set(r for r in refs if r not in labelset))
print("undefined refs:", undef or "none")

# brace balance in my three edited/new files
for f in ["sections/results.tex","sections/appendix.tex","sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex"]:
    t=texts[f]
    # ignore escaped braces
    tt=t.replace(r"\{","").replace(r"\}","")
    print(f"{f}: braces {{={tt.count('{')} }}={tt.count('}')} balanced={tt.count('{')==tt.count('}')}")
    for env in ["figure*","figure","itemize","deluxetable"]:
        b=t.count(r"\begin{"+env+"}"); e=t.count(r"\end{"+env+"}")
        if b or e: print(f"    {env}: begin={b} end={e} {'OK' if b==e else 'MISMATCH'}")

# is dsa_scint_acf.tex only \input (not double-included)?
print("\ndsa_scint_acf input count:", allt.count(r"\input{sections/dsa_scint_acf.tex}"))
print("dsa_lorentzian_summary include count:", allt.count("dsa_lorentzian_summary.pdf"))

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pypdfium2 as pdfium, os

def make_test(fontset, usetex_mathdefault=False, pdffonttype=42, tag=""):
    with plt.rc_context({"pdf.fonttype":pdffonttype, "mathtext.fontset":fontset,
                          "font.family":"serif"}):
        fig,ax=plt.subplots(figsize=(3,2))
        ax.set_ylabel(r"Decorrelation Bandwidth, $\gamma$ (MHz)")
        ax.set_title(r"$\nu_c$=1352 MHz  $\chi_r^2$=0.91")
        ax.plot([1,2],[1,2])
        f=f"_t_{tag}.pdf"; fig.savefig(f); plt.close(fig)
    t=pdfium.PdfDocument(f)[0].get_textpage().get_text_range()
    return t

for fs in ["cm","stix","stixsans","dejavuserif"]:
    t=make_test(fs, tag=fs)
    print(f"fontset={fs:12s} °={'°' in t} γ={'γ' in t} ν={'ν' in t} χ={'χ' in t}")
    print("   ->", repr(t[:90]))

import re
svg=open(base+"/wilhelm_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.svg").read()  # has γ, ν_c, χ²_r, ∝ν⁴
# find each <text> group and dump the ordered tspan chars with font
for m in re.finditer(r'<text\b[^>]*>(.*?)</text>', svg, re.S):
    block=m.group(1)
    chars=[]
    for tm in re.finditer(r"font-family:\s*'?([a-z0-9]+)'?[^>]*>(.*?)</tspan>", block, re.S):
        fam,ch=tm.group(1),tm.group(2)
        chars.append((fam, ch, hex(ord(ch)) if len(ch)==1 else ch))
    special=[c for c in chars if c[0] in ("cmmi10","cmsy10") and c[2] in ("0xb0","0xba","0xc2","0xa1")]
    if special:
        seq="".join(c[1] for c in chars)
        print("SEQ:", repr(seq[:40]), "| specials:", special)

import re, os, matplotlib
ttfdir=os.path.join(os.path.dirname(matplotlib.__file__),"mpl-data","fonts","ttf")
STIX_REG=os.path.join(ttfdir,"STIXGeneral.ttf")
STIX_ITA=os.path.join(ttfdir,"STIXGeneralItalic.ttf")

# codepoint -> (unicode char, is_italic)  [math variables italic; symbols upright]
CMMI={0xb0:("\u03b3",True), 0xba:("\u03bd",True), 0xc2:("\u03c7",True)}  # γ ν χ (italic math)
CMSY={0xa1:("\u2212",False), 0xbb:("\u221d",False)}  # − ∝  (upright symbols)

def remap_svg(text):
    # operate tspan-by-tspan: replace font-family cmmi10/cmsy10 spans' chars
    def fix_tspan(m):
        attrs, body = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        fam=None
        if "cmmi10" in attrs: fam,table="cmmi10",CMMI
        elif "cmsy10" in attrs: fam,table="cmsy10",CMSY
        else: return m.group(0)
        # replace each mapped char; set font-family to STIX (italic for cmmi vars)
        newbody="".join(table.get(ord(c),(c,None))[0] if len(c)==1 else c for c in body)
        # decide italic: cmmi vars -> italic STIX, cmsy -> upright STIX
        newfam="STIXGeneral:italic" if fam=="cmmi10" else "STIXGeneral"
        # swap the font-family token inside attrs
        newattrs=re.sub(r"font-family:\s*'?"+fam+r"'?", "font-family: '"+("STIXGeneral")+"'"+("; font-style: italic" if fam=="cmmi10" else ""), attrs)
        return "<tspan"+newattrs+">"+newbody+"</tspan>"
    return re.sub(r"<tspan([^>]*)>(.*?)</tspan>", fix_tspan, text, flags=re.S)

src=base+"/wilhelm_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.svg"
svg=open(src).read()
fixed=remap_svg(svg)
open("_wilhelm_fixed.svg","w").write(fixed)
# check no corrupted codepoints remain in cm spans
rem=re.findall(r"font-family:\s*'?(cmmi10|cmsy10)'?[^>]*>(.*?)</tspan>", fixed, re.S)
bad=[(f,c,hex(ord(c))) for f,b in rem for c in b if ord(c) in (0xb0,0xba,0xc2,0xa1,0xbb)]
print("remaining corrupted in cm spans:", bad or "none")
print("γ present:", "\u03b3" in fixed, "| ν:", "\u03bd" in fixed, "| χ:", "\u03c7" in fixed, "| −:", "\u2212" in fixed)

import pypdfium2 as pdfium
from svglib.svglib import svg2rlg
from reportlab.graphics import renderPDF
from reportlab.pdfbase import pdfmetrics
from reportlab.pdfbase.ttfonts import TTFont

# cmsy10 '/' (0x2f) is the ∝ glyph; 0xa1 is minus
CMSY={0xa1:("\u2212",False), 0x2f:("\u221d",False)}

def remap_svg(text):
    def fix_tspan(m):
        attrs, body = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        if "cmmi10" in attrs: fam,table="cmmi10",CMMI
        elif "cmsy10" in attrs: fam,table="cmsy10",CMSY
        else: return m.group(0)
        newbody="".join(table.get(ord(c),(c,None))[0] if len(c)==1 else c for c in body)
        newattrs=re.sub(r"font-family:\s*'?"+fam+r"'?",
                        "font-family: 'STIXGeneral'"+("; font-style: italic" if fam=="cmmi10" else ""),
                        attrs)
        return "<tspan"+newattrs+">"+newbody+"</tspan>"
    return re.sub(r"<tspan([^>]*)>(.*?)</tspan>", fix_tspan, text, flags=re.S)

# register STIX for reportlab
try:
    pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont("STIXGeneral", STIX_REG))
    pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont("STIXGeneral-Italic", STIX_ITA))

import pypdfium2 as pdfium
from PIL import Image
import numpy as np

# Build side-by-side comparison for 3 representative bursts (dense, sparse, single-freq)
checks=["zach","hamilton","freya"]
panels=[]
for b in checks:
    pdf=f"_fixed_pdf/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf"
    png=f"{base}/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.png"
    rend=pdfium.PdfDocument(pdf)[0].render(scale=1.6).to_pil().convert("RGB")
    ref=Image.open(png).convert("RGB")
    # match heights
    h=360
    rend=rend.resize((int(rend.width*h/rend.height),h))
    ref=ref.resize((int(ref.width*h/ref.height),h))
    row=Image.new("RGB",(rend.width+ref.width+20,h),"white")
    row.paste(ref,(0,0)); row.paste(rend,(ref.width+20,0))
    panels.append((b,row))
# stack
W=max(p.width for _,p in panels); H=sum(p.height for _,p in panels)+40*len(panels)
canvas=Image.new("RGB",(W,H),"white")
from PIL import ImageDraw
d=ImageDraw.Draw(canvas); y=0
for b,p in panels:
    d.text((5,y+2),f"{b}:  LEFT=committed PNG (truth)   RIGHT=fixed PDF render",fill="black")
    canvas.paste(p,(0,y+20)); y+=p.height+40
canvas.save("_compare_fixed_vs_committed.png")
print("saved comparison; sizes:", [(b,p.size) for b,p in panels])
stdout: saved comparison; sizes: [('zach', (749, 360)), ('hamilton', (749, 360)), ('freya', (1475, 360))]

import pypdfium2 as pdfium, numpy as np, os, re
from PIL import Image
from svglib.svglib import svg2rlg
from reportlab.graphics import renderPDF

base="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/figures"
bursts=["casey","chromatica","freya","hamilton","isha","johndoeII","mahi","oran","phineas","whitney","wilhelm","zach"]

def to_gray(img, size=(900,900)):
    return np.asarray(img.convert("L").resize(size), dtype=np.float32)/255.0

# Strategy:
# (1) Data-fidelity: render ORIGINAL committed SVG via svglib (same renderer as fixed) and
#     diff against fixed render. Any change must be confined to text-glyph pixels; if my
#     remap distorted layout/data, the diff spreads into panels. Report diff-fraction.
# (2) Renderer-fidelity: correlate fixed-PDF render against committed PNG (ground truth,
#     matplotlib-native). Different renderers => expect high but <1 corr; flag outliers.
os.makedirs("_orig_svg_pdf", exist_ok=True)
rows=[]
for b in bursts:
    # original committed SVG -> svglib PDF (unmodified glyphs, same renderer)
    orig_pdf=f"_orig_svg_pdf/{b}.pdf"
    renderPDF.drawToFile(svg2rlg(f"{base}/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.svg"), orig_pdf)
    g_orig=to_gray(pdfium.PdfDocument(orig_pdf)[0].render(scale=2.0).to_pil())
    g_fixed=to_gray(pdfium.PdfDocument(f"_fixed_pdf/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf")[0].render(scale=2.0).to_pil())
    g_png=to_gray(Image.open(f"{base}/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.png"))
    # (1) fraction of pixels that changed materially between orig-svg render and fixed render
    diff=np.abs(g_orig-g_fixed)
    changed_frac=float((diff>0.15).mean())
    # (2) normalized cross-correlation fixed-render vs committed PNG
    a=g_fixed-g_fixed.mean(); c=g_png-g_png.mean()
    corr=float((a*c).sum()/(np.sqrt((a*a).sum()*(c*c).sum())+1e-9))
    rows.append((b, changed_frac, corr))

print(f"{'burst':12s} {'changed_frac(remap)':>20s} {'corr_vs_PNG':>12s}")
for b,cf,co in rows:
    flag="" if (cf<0.02 and co>0.90) else "  <-- CHECK"
    print(f"{b:12s} {cf:>20.4f} {co:>12.4f}{flag}")
print("\nmax changed_frac:", round(max(r[1] for r in rows),4),
      "| min corr:", round(min(r[2] for r in rows),4))
stdout:

freya                      0.0006       0.7024  <-- CHECK

import pypdfium2 as pdfium, numpy as np
from PIL import Image

def to_gray(img, size): return np.asarray(img.convert("L").resize(size),dtype=np.float32)/255.0
def corr(a,b):
    a=a-a.mean(); b=b-b.mean()
    return float((a*b).sum()/(np.sqrt((a*a).sum()*(b*b).sum())+1e-9))

# Control on 3 bursts: does the ORIGINAL (unmodified) svglib render correlate with committed PNG
# at the SAME ~0.70 as the fixed render? If yes, 0.70 is a pure cross-renderer artifact.
# Also compute at native aspect ratio (no square squish) for a truthful metric.
for b in ["zach","hamilton","freya"]:
    png=Image.open(f"{base}/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.png").convert("L")
    sz=png.size  # native aspect
    g_png=to_gray(png, sz)
    g_orig=to_gray(pdfium.PdfDocument(f"_orig_svg_pdf/{b}.pdf")[0].render(scale=2.0).to_pil(), sz)
    g_fix =to_gray(pdfium.PdfDocument(f"_fixed_pdf/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf")[0].render(scale=2.0).to_pil(), sz)
    print(f"{b:10s} corr(orig_svglib,PNG)={corr(g_orig,g_png):.3f}  corr(fixed,PNG)={corr(g_fix,g_png):.3f}  corr(fixed,orig)={corr(g_fix,g_orig):.3f}")
stdout:
zach       corr(orig_svglib,PNG)=0.655  corr(fixed,PNG)=0.655  corr(fixed,orig)=0.998
hamilton   corr(orig_svglib,PNG)=0.669  corr(fixed,PNG)=0.670  corr(fixed,orig)=0.998
freya      corr(orig_svglib,PNG)=0.664  corr(fixed,PNG)=0.665  corr(fixed,orig)=0.998

skill_md = '''---
name: figure-pdf-text-qa
description: Verify and repair the embedded TEXT LAYER of matplotlib/vector PDF figures so math glyphs (gamma, nu, chi, minus, proportional) extract, search, copy, and read correctly. Use when a PDF figure renders visually correct but PDF text-search/copy/accessibility (e.g. Overleaf's index, screen readers) surfaces garbled glyphs like "degree sign" for gamma. Covers the Computer-Modern mathtext 0xb0 codepoint collision, the STIX-fontset fix at the matplotlib source, and an SVG codepoint-remap fallback when the figure cannot be regenerated. Always verify BOTH the extracted text layer AND a numeric raster comparison against ground truth before shipping.
---

# Figure PDF text-layer QA

A PDF figure can render **visually correct** while embedding a **garbled text
layer**. matplotlib's default Computer-Modern mathtext stores glyphs like
gamma at custom font codepoint `0xb0`, with no ToUnicode mapping, so any tool
that reads the PDF text stream (PDF search/copy, Overleaf's text index, screen
readers) extracts the wrong character (gamma -> degree sign). Raster/visual
inspection alone **cannot** catch this — you must extract and check the text.

## When to use
- Shipping a `.pdf` figure into a manuscript where text search / copy /
  accessibility matter.
- A reviewer/auditor reports garbled glyphs in a PDF that "looks fine".
- Converting SVG figures to PDF (svglib, cairosvg) — conversion often
  corrupts math glyphs.

## The correctness bar (both required)
1. **Text layer**: `check_text_layer(pdf)` — required glyphs extract correctly,
   forbidden ones absent.
2. **Raster fidelity**: a NUMERIC comparison against ground truth, not an
   eyeball. Never claim "pixel-for-pixel" or "verified each" without a metric
   over EVERY figure.

## Decision: regenerate vs remap
- **Prefer regenerate at the source.** If the figure's data is available
  (e.g. committed JSON with the plotted values), re-run the plotting code with
  the STIX mathtext fix — this yields a native, correct ToUnicode layer:
  ```python
  plt.rcParams.update({"mathtext.fontset": "stix", "pdf.fonttype": 42})
  ```
  `mathtext.fontset="stix"` keeps a serif look (good for AASTeX/Times
  manuscripts) while emitting correct unicode; `"cm"` cannot. Verify with the
  fontset probe: `probe_mathtext_fontsets()`.
- **Fallback: SVG codepoint remap.** If the figure embeds data you cannot
  regenerate (e.g. traces needing absent raw inputs) but a vector SVG exists,
  remap the corrupted Computer-Modern codepoints to real unicode with a STIX
  font, then render to PDF. Use `remap_cm_svg()` then svglib.

## Workflow

### 1. Diagnose
```python
info = check_text_layer("fig.pdf", required=["\\u03b3"], forbidden=["\\u00b0"])
# info["clean"] is False when gamma extracts as degree sign
```
Enumerate corrupted glyphs across SVGs with `find_corrupted_cm_glyphs(svg_dir)`.
The usual Computer-Modern culprits (confirm by context in YOUR figures):
- cmmi10 `0xb0`->gamma, `0xba`->nu, `0xc2`->chi   (math variables, italic)
- cmsy10 `0xa1`->minus (U+2212), `0x2f`->proportional (U+221D)  (symbols, upright)

### 2a. Regenerate (preferred)
Add the STIX rcParams to the plotting code and re-run from data. Then verify.

### 2b. Remap (fallback)
```python
CMMI = {0xb0:"\\u03b3", 0xba:"\\u03bd", 0xc2:"\\u03c7"}   # confirm per-figure
CMSY = {0xa1:"\\u2212", 0x2f:"\\u221d"}
fixed_svg = remap_cm_svg(open("in.svg").read(), CMMI, CMSY)
# then render fixed_svg -> pdf via svglib+reportlab with STIXGeneral registered
```

### 3. Verify BOTH, over EVERY figure
```python
check_text_layer(pdf, required=[...], forbidden=["\\u00b0","\\u00ba","\\u00c2","\\u00a1"])
# raster fidelity vs an original-glyph render through the SAME renderer:
m = raster_change_fraction(original_render_png_or_pdf, fixed_pdf)  # want << 1%
# and/or correlation vs ground-truth PNG, with a CONTROL: the unmodified
# original should show the same cross-renderer correlation — if so, a modest
# corr (~0.66 for noise-dense panels squished) is a renderer artifact, not
# distortion. See compare_renders().
```
Report the metric for all N figures. Do not generalize from a 3-of-12 sample.

## Pitfalls
- cairosvg corrupts CM math glyphs badly; svglib+reportlab renders them but
  copies the SAME broken text layer unless you remap first.
- A visual side-by-side is NOT a numeric check. Compute a fraction/correlation.
- Cross-renderer correlation (svglib vs matplotlib) is low on high-frequency
  noise; always run the unmodified-original control to attribute the number.
'''

res = host.skills.edit("figure-pdf-text-qa", "SKILL.md", skill_md)
print(res)

Verify with: cat /Users/jakobfaber/.claude-science/orgs/3dfe4daa-34a3-475b-a7d9-194ed338c8cc/skills/figure-pdf-text-qa/SKILL.md'}

kernel_py = r'''import os
import re

BAD_CM_GLYPHS = ("\u00b0", "\u00ba", "\u00c2", "\u00a1")


def check_text_layer(pdf_path, required=None, forbidden=None):
    """Extract a PDF's first-page text layer and check glyph correctness.

    required: list of unicode chars that MUST appear (e.g. ["\u03b3"]).
    forbidden: list of chars that must NOT appear (default: the common
    corrupted Computer-Modern glyphs). Returns a dict with the verdict.
    """
    import pypdfium2 as pdfium
    if forbidden is None:
        forbidden = list(BAD_CM_GLYPHS)
    required = required or []
    doc = pdfium.PdfDocument(pdf_path)
    text = doc[0].get_textpage().get_text_range()
    missing = [c for c in required if c not in text]
    present_bad = [c for c in forbidden if c in text]
    return {
        "pdf": pdf_path,
        "clean": (not missing) and (not present_bad),
        "missing_required": missing,
        "present_forbidden": present_bad,
        "text": text,
    }


def find_corrupted_cm_glyphs(svg_dir):
    """Scan SVGs for chars carried by cmmi10/cmsy10 font-family tspans.

    Returns a sorted set of (font_family, char, hexcodepoint). Latin letters
    usually extract fine; the high-codepoint entries are the corrupted ones.
    """
    import glob
    problem = set()
    for s in glob.glob(os.path.join(svg_dir, "*.svg")):
        svg = open(s).read()
        for fam in ("cmmi10", "cmsy10"):
            pat = r"font-family:\s*'?" + fam + r"'?[^>]*>(.*?)</tspan>"
            for m in re.finditer(pat, svg, re.S):
                for ch in m.group(1):
                    if ch.strip():
                        problem.add((fam, ch, hex(ord(ch))))
    return sorted(problem)


def remap_cm_svg(svg_text, cmmi_map=None, cmsy_map=None):
    """Remap corrupted Computer-Modern codepoints to real unicode + STIX.

    cmmi_map / cmsy_map: {int_codepoint: unicode_char}. cmmi glyphs (math
    variables) become italic STIXGeneral; cmsy glyphs (symbols) upright.
    Returns the fixed SVG string. Confirm the codepoint->glyph mapping by
    context in YOUR figures before trusting it.
    """
    if cmmi_map is None:
        cmmi_map = {0xb0: "\u03b3", 0xba: "\u03bd", 0xc2: "\u03c7"}
    if cmsy_map is None:
        cmsy_map = {0xa1: "\u2212", 0x2f: "\u221d"}

    def fix_tspan(m):
        attrs, body = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        if "cmmi10" in attrs:
            fam, table, italic = "cmmi10", cmmi_map, True
        elif "cmsy10" in attrs:
            fam, table, italic = "cmsy10", cmsy_map, False
        else:
            return m.group(0)
        newbody = "".join(
            table.get(ord(c), c) if len(c) == 1 else c for c in body
        )
        repl = "font-family: 'STIXGeneral'" + ("; font-style: italic" if italic else "")
        newattrs = re.sub(r"font-family:\s*'?" + fam + r"'?", repl, attrs)
        return "<tspan" + newattrs + ">" + newbody + "</tspan>"

    return re.sub(r"<tspan([^>]*)>(.*?)</tspan>", fix_tspan, svg_text, flags=re.S)


def stix_font_paths():
    """Return (regular, italic) paths to matplotlib's bundled STIXGeneral."""
    import matplotlib
    d = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(matplotlib.__file__), "mpl-data", "fonts", "ttf")
    return os.path.join(d, "STIXGeneral.ttf"), os.path.join(d, "STIXGeneralItalic.ttf")


def render_svg_to_pdf(svg_path, pdf_path):
    """Render an SVG to PDF via svglib+reportlab with STIXGeneral registered."""
    from svglib.svglib import svg2rlg
    from reportlab.graphics import renderPDF
    from reportlab.pdfbase import pdfmetrics
    from reportlab.pdfbase.ttfonts import TTFont
    reg, ita = stix_font_paths()
    try:
        pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont("STIXGeneral", reg))
        pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont("STIXGeneral-Italic", ita))
    except Exception:
        pass
    renderPDF.drawToFile(svg2rlg(svg_path), pdf_path)
    return pdf_path


def _to_gray(img, size):
    import numpy as np
    return np.asarray(img.convert("L").resize(size), dtype="float32") / 255.0


def probe_mathtext_fontsets(fontsets=None):
    """Render a gamma/nu/chi test label under each fontset; report which
    emit a clean unicode PDF text layer. Returns {fontset: dict}."""
    import matplotlib
    matplotlib.use("Agg")
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import pypdfium2 as pdfium
    import tempfile
    if fontsets is None:
        fontsets = ["cm", "stix", "stixsans", "dejavuserif"]
    out = {}
    for fs in fontsets:
        with plt.rc_context({"pdf.fonttype": 42, "mathtext.fontset": fs,
                             "font.family": "serif"}):
            fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(3, 2))
            ax.set_ylabel(r"Bandwidth, $\gamma$ (MHz)")
            ax.set_title(r"$\nu_c$  $\chi_r^2$")
            ax.plot([1, 2], [1, 2])
            f = tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".pdf", delete=False).name
            fig.savefig(f)
            plt.close(fig)
        t = pdfium.PdfDocument(f)[0].get_textpage().get_text_range()
        out[fs] = {"gamma": "\u03b3" in t, "nu": "\u03bd" in t,
                   "chi": "\u03c7" in t, "degree_bad": "\u00b0" in t}
    return out


def raster_change_fraction(ref_pdf, test_pdf, scale=2.0, thresh=0.15):
    """Fraction of pixels differing by > thresh between two PDF renders.

    Render both first pages, size-match, return the fraction changed. For a
    text-only fix, feed the ORIGINAL-glyph render (same renderer) as ref: a
    correct remap leaves this << 0.01 (only glyph pixels move)."""
    import numpy as np
    import pypdfium2 as pdfium
    a_img = pdfium.PdfDocument(ref_pdf)[0].render(scale=scale).to_pil()
    b_img = pdfium.PdfDocument(test_pdf)[0].render(scale=scale).to_pil()
    size = a_img.size
    a = _to_gray(a_img, size)
    b = _to_gray(b_img, size)
    return float((np.abs(a - b) > thresh).mean())


def compare_renders(pdf_a, pdf_b, scale=2.0, size=None):
    """Normalized cross-correlation between two PDF first-page renders.

    Use to compare a fixed PDF against a ground-truth image render. Pair with
    a CONTROL (unmodified original vs same ground truth) to attribute a low
    value to cross-renderer differences rather than distortion."""
    import numpy as np
    import pypdfium2 as pdfium
    ia = pdfium.PdfDocument(pdf_a)[0].render(scale=scale).to_pil()
    ib = pdfium.PdfDocument(pdf_b)[0].render(scale=scale).to_pil()
    if size is None:
        size = ia.size
    a = _to_gray(ia, size)
    b = _to_gray(ib, size)
    a = a - a.mean()
    b = b - b.mean()
    return float((a * b).sum() / ((a * a).sum() ** 0.5 * (b * b).sum() ** 0.5 + 1e-9))
'''

res = host.skills.edit("figure-pdf-text-qa", "kernel.py", kernel_py)
print(res.get("action"), "| gate:", res.get("sidecar_gate"), "| note:", res.get("note"))

Fix and re-edit; publish will refuse until the gate passes.

import pypdfium2 as pdfium, os
figdir="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/figures"
bursts=["casey","chromatica","freya","hamilton","isha","johndoeII","mahi","oran","phineas","whitney","wilhelm","zach"]
def verdict(p):
    t=pdfium.PdfDocument(p)[0].get_textpage().get_text_range()
    bad=any(ch in t for ch in ("°","º","\u00c2","\u00a1"))
    return ("CLEAN" if (not bad and "γ" in t) else "GARBLED"), ("°" in t)
s=f"{figdir}/dsa_lorentzian_summary.pdf"
print("summary:", verdict(s), "mtime", round(os.path.getmtime(s)))
allclean=verdict(s)[0]=="CLEAN"
for b in bursts:
    p=f"{figdir}/dsa_scint_acf/{b}_dsa_acf_lorentzian_fits.pdf"
    v=verdict(p); allclean=allclean and v[0]=="CLEAN"
    print(f"  {b:12s} {v[0]}")
print("\nALL 13 manuscript figures clean:", allclean)

=== HANDOFF_SCINT_DATA_PRODUCTS.md ===
# HANDOFF — Generate all data products needed to complete the scintillation analysis

**Date:** 2026-07-07. **Author:** audit session (Claude + JF).
**Goal:** produce a pipeline-ready dynamic-spectrum npz for **every burst × both bands**
(12 CHIME + 12 DSA = 24 products), so the shared ACF pipeline
(`scint_analysis/pipeline.py`, `analyze_scintillation` / `analyze_scintillation_from_acfs`)
can be run uniformly across the full sample. Same algorithm both bands: frequency ACF →
Lorentzian / gen-Lorentzian / composite fits, BIC + nested-F component selection,
sub-band Δν_d(ν) power law → α, NE2025 Galactic floor.

> ⚠ `DATA_PROVENANCE.md` is **stale on paths** (verified 2026-07-07): `h17:/data/jfaber/`
> was emptied ~2026-06-27 by the arc_cleanup/migrate scripts. Everything now lives under
> `h17:/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/` (call it `$COD` below).
> Trust the paths in THIS document; update DATA_PROVENANCE.md as part of this task.

---

## 1. The sample (12 bursts)

| nickname | TNS | CHIME event ID | DSA date code |

(oran and johndoeII IDs were the two missing entries in DATA_PROVENANCE.md §7b — resolved
by `$COD/dsa_filterbanks/Codetections_DSA_Filterbanks/<nick>_<datecode>_<eventid>/` naming.)

---

## 2. Current inventory (verified on disk 2026-07-07)

### CHIME band
- **h17, complete for all 12:** `$COD/upchan_codetections/{nick}_chime_upchan.npy` +
  `{nick}_chime_freq.npy` + `{nick}_time0_metadata.json`, regenerated 2026-07-06/07
  (`regen_20260706.log`, `regen_batch2_20260707.log`). Quarantined bad runs:
  `DEFECTIVE_nodedisp_20260703/`, `SUPERSEDED_timeshift_20260704/` — do NOT use.
- **Local Mac, packaged npz for 6/12:** `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/scintillation-data/`
  has `{casey,freya,isha,mahi,phineas,whitney}_chime.npz` (+ `_hi.npz`), built 2026-07-06.
  **Missing locally: chromatica, hamilton, wilhelm, zach, johndoeII, oran.**
- Packaging scripts + provenance: `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/upchan_codetections/`
  (`build_npz_aligned_generic_20260706.py`, `PROVENANCE.md`).

### DSA band
- **Local packaged npz: 1/12** — `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/scintillation/data/freya.npz`
  (57 MB, 2026-07-04; symlinked into `scintillation-data/`). Other 11 absent from Mac.
- **CANFAR (authoritative): all 12** — `$ARC/FLITS/scintillation/data/{nick}.npz`,
  `$ARC = arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/`.
- **h17 raw filterbanks: all 12 (+ extras)** —
  `$COD/dsa_filterbanks/Codetections_DSA_Filterbanks/<nick>_<datecode>_<id>/<datecode>_dev_polcal_I.fil`.
- **Local raw waterfalls: all 12** — `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/DSA_bursts/{nick}_dsa_I_*_2500b_cntr_bpc.npy`
  (centered, bandpass-corrected Stokes-I; preprocessing lineage vs CANFAR npz unverified).
- **Pipeline-ready DSA ACF products (3):**
  `h17:$COD/archive/arc_trash_2026-06/acf_results/{chromatica,freya,wilhelm}_acf_results.pkl`
  (⚠ sitting in an `arc_trash` dir — copy out before any further cleanup deletes them).

### Fit products already committed in repo
- `configs/bursts/freya_dsa.yaml`, `wilhelm_dsa.yaml` — `stored_fits` (keep; will be
  re-validated, not regenerated).
- `chime_acfs/{chromatica,freya,hamilton,wilhelm}_{id}_subband_acf_fits.pkl` — legacy
  fit products (`1_lorenz`/`2_lorenz` schema), NOT re-fittable under the BIC selector;
  superseded by the fresh upchannelization. Keep for cross-checks only.
- `freya_analysis_results.json` — **degenerate** (bw_at_ref=3.84e12 MHz, α=4.0±0.0,
  empty subbands). Do not cite; regenerate.

---

## 3. DSA npz: pull from CANFAR (recommended) — NOT regenerate from .fil

**Decision: `vcp` the 11 missing npz from CANFAR.** Rationale:
1. The CANFAR npz are the exact files the 12 `configs/bursts/{nick}_dsa.yaml` already

=== rest of handoff: fitting/alpha section ===
1. The CANFAR npz are the exact files the 12 `configs/bursts/{nick}_dsa.yaml` already
   point to (`${FLITS_ROOT}/scintillation/data/{nick}.npz`) — schema guaranteed to match
   what the pipeline consumes, and consistent with the already-committed freya/wilhelm fits.
2. Regenerating from `.fil` would require reverse-engineering the original preprocessing
   (on-pulse selection, RFI mask, bandpass) and would create a second DSA lineage —
   exactly the legacy-vs-new fork we're trying to eliminate on the CHIME side.
3. Transfer cost trivial: ~12 × O(50–120 MB).

Fallback only if CANFAR is unreachable: package `.fil` (h17) or the local
`DSA_bursts/*.npy` via a build script, then **validate against `freya.npz`**
(same shape/keys/values) before trusting.

**How (verified pattern, run from h17 inside the baseband container with the CADC proxy):**
```bash
ssh h17
docker run --rm -it \
  -v /data/research:/data/research \
  -v ~/.ssl:/root/.ssl:ro \
  chimefrb/baseband-analysis:latest bash
# inside container:
for n in casey whitney phineas mahi isha chromatica hamilton wilhelm zach johndoeII oran; do
  vcp arc:home/jfaber/baseband_morphologies/chime_dsa_codetections/FLITS/scintillation/data/$n.npz \
      /data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/results/dsa_scint_npz/
done
```
Then rsync `dsa_scint_npz/` to the Mac →
`~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/scintillation/data/` (where `freya.npz` already lives).

**Immediately also:** copy the 3 `*_acf_results.pkl` out of
`archive/arc_trash_2026-06/acf_results/` into `results/` (they are one cleanup pass
away from deletion).

---

## 4. CHIME npz: package the remaining 6 bursts

Inputs already exist on h17 (all 12 regenerated Jul 6–7). Local packaging pipeline
already worked for 6 bursts on Jul 6 — repeat for the rest:

1. Pull raw upchan products for **chromatica, hamilton, wilhelm, zach, johndoeII, oran**:
   ```bash
   for n in chromatica hamilton wilhelm zach johndoeII oran; do
     rsync -avP h17:/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/upchan_codetections/${n}_chime_{upchan,freq}.npy \
                h17:/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections/upchan_codetections/${n}_time0_metadata.json \
                ~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/upchan_codetections/
   done
   ```
2. Package with `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/upchan_codetections/build_npz_aligned_generic_20260706.py`
   (read its `PROVENANCE.md` first; it synthesizes `times_s` from time0 metadata —
   this is the fix for the DEFECTIVE/SUPERSEDED runs). Output
   `{nick}_chime.npz` (+ `_hi.npz`) → `~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/scintillation-data/`.
3. Sanity check each npz against `casey_chime.npz` (same keys, freq axis 400–800 MHz,
   expected upchan resolution 0.390625/U MHz).

---

## 5. Configs to create/verify (repo: `pipeline/scintillation/configs/bursts/`)

- DSA: all 12 `{nick}_dsa.yaml` exist. Verify each `data_path` resolves to the
  newly-staged npz (set `FLITS_ROOT` or edit paths). Do NOT clobber `stored_fits`
  in freya/wilhelm.

=== does a CHIME config differ? (freya_chime) ===
burst_id: freya
input_data_path: ${FLITS_ROOT}/scintillation/data/freya_chime.npz
telescope: chime
analysis:
  rfi_masking:
    find_burst_thres: 8.0
    padding_factor: 0.0
    use_symmetric_noise_window: true
    rfi_downsample_factor: 16
    enable_time_domain_flagging: false
    time_threshold_sigma: 7.0
    freq_threshold_sigma: 5.0
    # time bins (dt=0.32768 ms): v3 product (2026-07-04, --no-time-shift de-chirp
    # + metadata-aligned builder on a padded 446-bin canvas, no circular wraps —
    # low band fully usable, z 10-13 per 25 MHz slice at 400-500). Burst peak bin
    # 254, >3 sigma span 253-263 in 600-800; low-band peak lags by ~0.3 tau(nu) +
    # the 0.07 pc/cm3 DM offset (912.4 used vs CHIME structure DM 912.4699), up
    # to ~7 bins at 412 MHz — window padded to 268 to hold the full scattered
    # envelope. Known narrowband RFI burst at 482-487 MHz around bin 391 (masked
    # by freq-domain RFI stage). Noise window starts at 10 (leading offsets 0-9).
    manual_burst_window:
    - 253
    - 268
    manual_noise_window:
    - 10
    - 200
  acf:
    # Full CHIME band is diagnostic only: the expected NE2025 MW floor is ~38.8 kHz at
    # 600 MHz and scales as nu^4.4 to ~138 kHz at 800 MHz, while the 400 MHz scale is
    # near one 6.1 kHz fine channel and is effectively unresolved.

echo "=== any *.npz named like scint anywhere under COD (capped) ==="
find {COD} -maxdepth 3 -name "*.npz" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "scint\|_dsa\|_chime" | head -30
echo "=== CHIME upchan products ==="; ls {COD}/upchan_codetections/*_chime_upchan.npy 2>/dev/null | wc -l

echo "=== conda envs / flits checkout on h17? ==="; conda env list 2>/dev/null | head; ls -d ~/*FLITS* ~/*flits* /data/research/*/FLITS 2>/dev/null | head
'''
r = c.call_command(cmd, intent="Probe h17 for staged CHIME/DSA scintillation npz inventory (read-only ls/find)", login_shell=True)
print("exit:", r.get("exit_code"))
print(r.get("stdout","")[:4000])
print("--- stderr tail ---", r.get("stderr","")[-500:])

# ==============================================================================

# ==============================================================================
import argparse
import json
import logging

import numpy as np

from . import config, pipeline, plotting


class NumpyJSONEncoder(json.JSONEncoder):
    """
    Custom JSON encoder for NumPy data types.
    This converts NumPy types to standard Python types for JSON serialization.
    """

    def default(self, obj):
        if isinstance(obj, np.integer):
            return int(obj)
        elif isinstance(obj, np.floating):
            return float(obj)
        elif isinstance(obj, np.ndarray):
            return obj.tolist()
        return super().default(obj)


def main():
    """
    Main function to run the scintillation analysis pipeline from the command line.
    """
    # 1. Set up Command-Line Argument Parser
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Run a scintillation analysis pipeline on FRB data."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "burst_config_path", type=str, help="Path to the burst-specific YAML configuration file."
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

=== npy_to_npz.py (CHIME packaging candidate) ===
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Convert a recovered CHIME upchannelized Stokes-I .npy into the .npz the FLITS scint pipeline reads.

The pipeline's DynamicSpectrum.from_numpy_file expects keys power_2d / frequencies_mhz / times_s, and
it does `np.flip(power_2d, axis=0)` on load then re-flips to ascending iff frequencies are descending.
Net: storing power_2d freq-DESCENDING + frequencies_mhz ASCENDING lands aligned-ascending. The worker
already wrote <name>_chime_upchan.npy ascending, so we flip the rows back to descending here.

  python npy_to_npz.py casey --U 16    # -> scintillation/data/casey_chime.npz
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np

CHIME_NATIVE_DT_S = 2.56e-6  # time bin after upchannelizing = fftsize * native = 2*U * 2.56us
HERE = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
REPO = HERE.parents[2]


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__.split("\n", 1)[0])
    p.add_argument("name")
    p.add_argument(
        "--U", type=int, required=True, help="upchannelization factor used (dt = 2*U*2.56us)"
    )
    p.add_argument("--products", default=str(HERE / "products"))
    p.add_argument("--out", default=None)
    p.add_argument(
        "--fmin", type=float, default=None, help="keep only freq >= fmin MHz (high-band focus)"
    )
    p.add_argument("--fmax", type=float, default=None, help="keep only freq <= fmax MHz")
    args = p.parse_args(argv)

    prod = Path(args.products)
    stokes = np.load(prod / f"{args.name}_chime_upchan.npy")  # (n_freq, n_time), freq ascending
    freq = np.load(prod / f"{args.name}_chime_freq.npy")  # MHz, ascending
    assert stokes.shape[0] == freq.size, f"freq/spec mismatch {stokes.shape} vs {freq.shape}"

    sliced = args.fmin is not None or args.fmax is not None
    if sliced:
        keep = np.ones(freq.size, bool)
        if args.fmin is not None:
            keep &= freq >= args.fmin
        if args.fmax is not None:
            keep &= freq <= args.fmax
        assert keep.sum() >= 256, f"slice keeps only {keep.sum()} fine channels"

assert keep.sum() >= 256, f"slice keeps only {keep.sum()} fine channels"
        stokes, freq = stokes[keep], freq[keep]

    dt = 2 * args.U * CHIME_NATIVE_DT_S
    times_s = np.arange(stokes.shape[1]) * dt

    suffix = "_chime_hi" if sliced else "_chime"
    out = (
        Path(args.out) if args.out else REPO / "scintillation" / "data" / f"{args.name}{suffix}.npz"
    )
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    # power_2d freq-descending + frequencies_mhz ascending -> loader aligns to ascending.
    np.savez(out, power_2d=stokes[::-1, :], frequencies_mhz=freq, times_s=times_s)

    # ponytail self-check: reload through the pipeline's own loader and confirm alignment + shape.
    import sys

    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO / "scintillation" / "scint_analysis"))
    from core import DynamicSpectrum  # noqa: PLC0415

    ds = DynamicSpectrum.from_numpy_file(str(out))
    assert ds.power.shape == stokes.shape, f"roundtrip shape {ds.power.shape} != {stokes.shape}"
    assert ds.frequencies[0] < ds.frequencies[-1], "frequencies not ascending after load"
    # the loaded power row 0 (lowest freq) must equal our ascending stokes row 0
    lo = ds.power[0].filled(np.nan) if np.ma.isMaskedArray(ds.power) else ds.power[0]
    assert np.allclose(np.nan_to_num(lo), np.nan_to_num(stokes[0]), atol=0), "freq/power misaligned"
    print(
        f"[{args.name}] {out.name}: power {ds.power.shape}, "
        f"{ds.frequencies[0]:.1f}-{ds.frequencies[-1]:.1f} MHz, dt={dt * 1e3:.4f} ms, n_time={times_s.size}"
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

{VENV}/bin/python - <<'PY'
import numpy as np, json
COD="{COD}"
bursts=["casey","chromatica","freya","hamilton","isha","johndoeII","mahi","oran","phineas","whitney","wilhelm","zach"]
# native CHIME fine channel = 0.390625 MHz; upchan resolution = 0.390625/U... actually freq bins tell U:
# native n_freq=1024 over 400-800; upchan n_freq = 1024*U. So U = n_freq/1024.
for n in bursts:
    fr=np.load(f"{{COD}}/upchan_codetections/{{n}}_chime_freq.npy")
    nf=fr.size
    U_freq = nf/1024.0
    df = np.median(np.abs(np.diff(fr)))
    print(f"{{n:12s}} n_freq={{nf:6d}} U(from n_freq/1024)={{U_freq:6.2f}} df={{df*1000:.4f}} kHz")
PY
'''
r = c.call_command(cmd, intent="Compute upchan factor U per burst from freq array size (read-only)", login_shell=True, timeout_seconds=120)
print("exit:", r.get("exit_code"))
print(r.get("stdout","")[:2000])

=== run_analysis.py full main ===
    args = parser.parse_args()

    # 2. Load Configuration
    try:
        loaded_config = config.load_config(args.burst_config_path)
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: Could not load configuration. {e}")
        return

    # 3. Set up Logging
    log_level = loaded_config.get("pipeline_options", {}).get("log_level", "INFO").upper()
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=log_level,
        format="%(asctime)s - %(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s",
        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    )

    # 4. Initialize and Run the Pipeline
    # Resolve fallback paths for helper configs if not provided in YAML
    # This mirrors robust resolution used by the scattering CLI.
    from pathlib import Path as _P

    base_dir = _P(args.burst_config_path).parent

    def _resolve_cfg(base_dir: _P, filename: str) -> _P:
        cands = [
            base_dir / filename,
            base_dir.parent / filename,
            base_dir.parent.parent / filename,
        ]
        for c in cands:
            if c.exists():
                return c
        return cands[-1]

    loaded_config.setdefault("pipeline_options", {})
    # no explicit helper files here, but ensure any relative paths are rooted
    if "input_data_path" in loaded_config:
        loaded_config["input_data_path"] = str(_P(loaded_config["input_data_path"]))

    scint_pipeline = pipeline.ScintillationAnalysis(loaded_config)
    scint_pipeline.run()

    # 5. Handle and Save Results
    if not scint_pipeline.final_results:
        logging.error("Pipeline did not produce any results.")
        return

    # Save results to a JSON file using the custom encoder
    burst_id = loaded_config.get("burst_id", "output")
    output_path = f"./{burst_id}_analysis_results.json"
    try:
        with open(output_path, "w") as f:
            json.dump(scint_pipeline.final_results, f, indent=4, cls=NumpyJSONEncoder)
        logging.info(f"Final results successfully saved to {output_path}")
    except TypeError as e:
        logging.error(f"Could not serialize results to JSON. {e}")

    # 6. Generate Final Plots
    if scint_pipeline.acf_results and scint_pipeline.all_subband_fits:
        logging.info("Generating final analysis overview plot...")
        plotting.plot_analysis_overview(
            scint_pipeline.final_results,
            scint_pipeline.acf_results,
            scint_pipeline.all_subband_fits,
            scint_pipeline.all_powerlaw_fits,
        )
    else:
        logging.warning("Intermediate results not available, skipping overview plot.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

cd /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline/scintillation/scint_analysis
echo "=== final_results assembly + fit_2d block ==="

stdout:
=== final_results assembly + fit_2d block ===
            log.info("Running 2D global scintillation fit across all sub-bands...")
            self.fit_2d_result = self._run_2d_scintillation_fit(fit_2d_config)

        log.info("--- Pipeline execution finished. ---")

    def _run_2d_scintillation_fit(self, fit_2d_config):
        """
        Run 2D global scintillation fit across all sub-bands.

        This enforces physical frequency scaling: γ(ν) = γ₀ × (ν/ν_ref)^α
        and provides direct measurement of the scaling index α.
        """
        try:
            from .fitting_2d import Scintillation2DResult, fit_2d_scintillation
        except ImportError as e:
            log.warning(f"Could not import fitting_2d module: {e}. Skipping 2D fit.")
            return None

        if self.acf_results is None or not self.acf_results.get("subband_acfs"):
            log.warning("No ACF results available for 2D fitting.")
            return None

        try:
            result = fit_2d_scintillation(
                self.acf_results,
                model_type=fit_2d_config.get("model_type", "lorentzian"),
                fit_range_mhz=fit_2d_config.get("fit_range_mhz", 25.0),
                nu_ref=fit_2d_config.get("nu_ref", None),
                gamma_0_init=fit_2d_config.get("gamma_0_init", 1.0),
                alpha_init=fit_2d_config.get("alpha_init", 4.0),
                m_0_init=fit_2d_config.get("m_0_init", 0.5),
                vary_alpha=fit_2d_config.get("vary_alpha", True),
                include_self_noise=fit_2d_config.get("include_self_noise", False),
            )

            log.info(
                f"2D fit complete: γ₀ = {result.gamma_0:.3f} ± {result.gamma_0_err:.3f} MHz, "
                f"α = {result.alpha:.2f} ± {result.alpha_err:.2f}, "
                f"χ²_red = {result.redchi:.2f}"
            )

            # Store in final_results for convenience
            if self.final_results is not None:
                self.final_results["fit_2d"] = {
                    "gamma_0": result.gamma_0,
                    "gamma_0_err": result.gamma_0_err,
                    "alpha": result.alpha,
                    "alpha_err": result.alpha_err,
                    "m_0": result.m_0,
                    "m_0_err": result.m_0_err,
                    "nu_ref": result.nu_ref,
                    "redchi": result.redchi,
                    "success": result.success,
                }

            return result

        except Exception as e:
            log.error(f"2D scintillation fit failed: {e}")
            return None

source:
cat > /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/run_dualband.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#SBATCH --time=90
#SBATCH --partition=main
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=12
#SBATCH --mem=32G

set -eo pipefail
COD=/data/research/astrophysics/frbs/chime-dsa-codetections
VENV=/home/ubuntu/scratch/flits-venv
PY=$VENV/bin/python
CODE=$PWD/code
FR=$PWD/flits_root
export FLITS_ROOT=$FR
export NUMBA_DISABLE_JIT=1
export PYTHONPATH=$CODE/scintillation

# 1. unpack code
mkdir -p $CODE && tar xzf ./flits_code.tgz -C $CODE
echo "[stage] code unpacked"

# 2. FLITS_ROOT/scintillation/data with DSA npz + packaged CHIME npz
mkdir -p $FR/scintillation/data
for f in $COD/results/dsa_scint_npz/*.npz; do ln -sf "$f" $FR/scintillation/data/$(basename "$f"); done
echo "[stage] DSA npz linked: $(ls $FR/scintillation/data/*.npz | wc -l)"

# 3. package 12 CHIME npz from upchan .npy (U from channel width)
BURSTS="casey chromatica freya hamilton isha johndoeII mahi oran phineas whitney wilhelm zach"
NPZ2NPY=$CODE/analysis/scattering-refit-2026-06/baseband_recovery/npy_to_npz.py
for n in $BURSTS; do
  # U = round(390.625 kHz / df_kHz); compute in python, package
  $PY - "$n" <<PYEOF
import numpy as np, sys, subprocess, os
n=sys.argv[1]
COD=os.environ.get("COD","$COD")
prod="$COD/upchan_codetections"
fr=np.load(f"{prod}/{n}_chime_freq.npy")
df=np.median(np.abs(np.diff(fr)))*1000.0  # kHz
U=int(round(390.625/df))
out=f"$FR/scintillation/data/{n}_chime.npz"
rc=subprocess.run(["$PY","$NPZ2NPY",n,"--U",str(U),"--products",prod,"--out",out],
                  capture_output=True,text=True)
print(f"[pkg] {n} U={U} df={df:.4f}kHz rc={rc.returncode} {rc.stdout.strip()[:120]} {rc.stderr.strip()[-200:]}")
PYEOF
done
echo "[stage] CHIME npz packaged: $(ls $FR/scintillation/data/*_chime.npz | wc -l)"

# 4. run pipeline for all 24 configs (12 dsa + 12 chime base)
mkdir -p ./out
CFG=$CODE/scintillation/configs/bursts
run_one () {
  local cfg=$1 tag=$2
  cd ./out
  if $PY -m scint_analysis.run_analysis "$cfg" > ${tag}.log 2>&1; then
    echo "[run] $tag OK"
  else
    echo "[run] $tag FAILED (see ${tag}.log)"
  fi
  cd ..
}
export -f run_one
export PY FR CODE
for n in $BURSTS; do
  run_one "$CFG/${n}_dsa.yaml"   "${n}_dsa"
  run_one "$CFG/${n}_chime.yaml" "${n}_chime"
done

# 5. collect results
cp ./out/*_analysis_results.json ./ 2>/dev/null || true
echo "[done] result jsons: $(ls ./*_analysis_results.json 2>/dev/null | wc -l)"
ls -la ./*_analysis_results.json 2>/dev/null | head -30
# tar logs for diagnostics
tar czf pipeline_logs.tgz ./out/*.log 2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT
echo "written"; wc -l /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/run_dualband.sh
stdout:
written
      71 /Users/jakobfaber/Developer/scratch/run_dualband.sh

import numpy as np, subprocess

U=int(round(390.625/df))
rc=subprocess.run(["$PY","$NPZ2NPY","freya","--U",str(U),"--products","$COD/upchan_codetections","--out","$FR/scintillation/data/freya_chime.npz"],capture_output=True,text=True)
print("PKG U=",U,"rc=",rc.returncode,rc.stdout.strip(),rc.stderr.strip()[-300:])
PYEOF
ls -la $FR/scintillation/data/

mkdir -p ./out && cd ./out

for tag in freya_dsa freya_chime; do
  echo "=== running $tag ==="

done
cd ..
cp ./out/*_analysis_results.json ./ 2>/dev/null || true
echo "=== result jsons ==="; ls -la ./*_analysis_results.json 2>/dev/null
tar czf smoke_logs.tgz ./out/*.log 2>/dev/null || true
SCRIPT

c = host.compute.create('ssh:h17')
# submit a tiny job that just stages the b64 file and reports its md5 + decodes it
job = c.submit_job(
    command='''#SBATCH --time=5
#SBATCH --partition=main
set -eo pipefail
echo "b64 size:"; wc -c flits_code.b64
python3 -c "import base64,hashlib; d=base64.b64decode(open('flits_code.b64').read()); open('flits_code.tgz','wb').write(d); print('decoded md5:', hashlib.md5(d).hexdigest(), 'bytes:', len(d))"
gzip -t flits_code.tgz && echo GZIP_OK || echo GZIP_BAD
tar tzf flits_code.tgz | wc -l
cp flits_code.tgz ./staged_code.tgz
''',
    intent="Staging integrity test: transfer base64 code, decode, verify md5+gzip on h17 — 1 min",
    inputs=[{"src": "flits_code.b64", "dst_filename": "flits_code.b64"}],
    outputs=[{"glob": "staged_code.tgz", "visibility": "hidden"}],
    timeout_seconds=300,
)
print("job_id:", job.job_id)
